Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log of several cases C001 to C005 with various attributes and events The task is to identify where and how bias manifests in the process focusing on attributes and adjustments that favor certain groups

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: CommunityGroup Some applications have a CommunityGroup eg Highland Civic Darts Club and some have None

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: LocalResident This is a boolean TRUE FALSE In the log we see both TRUE and FALSE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: ScoreAdjustment Some entries have adjustments eg plus 10 Community and others have 0 or NA

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The adjustment plus 10 Community only appears for cases that have a CommunityGroup C001 C004 and for which LocalResident is TRUE Cases without a CommunityGroup C002 C003 C005 have adjustment 0 or NA

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Specifically C001 PreliminaryScoring row has adjustment plus 10 Community C002 PreliminaryScoring row has adjustment 0 C003 PreliminaryScoring row has adjustment 0 C004 PreliminaryScoring row has adjustment plus 10 Community C005 PreliminaryScoring row has adjustment 0

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The PreliminaryScore is then adjusted in the ManualReview step for C001 and C004 they show 720 Adjusted and 700 Adjusted respectively but the adjustment reason is the same plus 10 Community

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Without the adjustment C004 would have 690 and might have been rejected But note that the threshold for approval is not stated

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can compare C002 Score 720 no adjustment approved C003 Score 715 no adjustment rejected C005 Score 740 no adjustment approved So it seems that without adjustment the threshold might be around 720

Name: Validation - IND
Text: However we cannot be sure because there might be other factors

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The key point is that the adjustment of plus 10 for community members can push an application over the threshold

Name: Validation - PE
Text: C001 Without the adjustment the PreliminaryScore was 710 then adjusted by plus 10 to 720 Then approved C004 Without the adjustment the PreliminaryScore was 690 then adjusted by plus 10 to 700 Then approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Being a non local resident without a community group does not get the plus 10 adjustment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is that the process gives an extra 10 points to applicants who are local residents and part of a community group This adjustment is not available to non local residents or those without a community group

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Unfair advantage for community members The adjustment of plus 10 for community members can make the difference between approval and rejection

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: An applicant with the same preliminary score but without a community group would remain at that score and be rejected while a community member could receive the extra points and possibly be approved

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Geographic bias The adjustment is only given to local residents This is a bias against non local residents

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The adjustment is based on factors that are not directly related to creditworthiness Being part of a community group and being a local resident might be correlated with socioeconomic status race or other factors

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This adjustment could therefore perpetuate existing inequalities

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The adjustment is labeled Community but the exact criteria for what constitutes a community group and why it warrants a plus 10 adjustment is not clear

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The adjustment can change the outcome We see that C004 with 690 becomes 700 and is approved Without the adjustment it would have been rejected Similarly C001 with 710 becomes 720 and is approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias manifests in the ScoreAdjustment step specifically the plus 10 Community adjustment which is only applied to applicants who are local residents and have a community group

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This adjustment favors local residents and applicants who are part of a community group

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This creates an unfair advantage for the favored groups and can lead to approvals for applicants who might not have been approved based on their PreliminaryScore alone Conversely applicants without these affiliations might be rejected despite having a PreliminaryScore that is close to the threshold

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The process is not fair because it uses attributes community group and local residency that are not directly related to creditworthiness and may be correlated with socioeconomic or demographic factors
